Genesis.title: Genesis
Genesis.summary: Genesis tells the story of God's good creation, humanity's fall into sin, and God's covenant promises to rescue and bless the world through the family of Abraham.
keyThemes: Covenant promises to Abraham and his descendants

Mark.title: Gospel of Mark
Mark.summary: Mark presents Jesus as the suffering Servant and Son of God, moving quickly through His ministry with a focus on what He does and His journey to the cross.
keyThemes: The urgency of repentance and faith

Exodus.title: Exodus
Exodus.summary: Exodus tells the story of how God's people, the Israelites, were enslaved in Egypt and how God used Moses to lead them to freedom. It shows how God made a covenant with His people and gave them the Ten Commandments. The book highlights God's power, faithfulness, and desire for His people to live according to His laws.

Leviticus.title: Leviticus
Leviticus.summary: Leviticus is a book that explains the laws and rules given to the Israelites for worshipping God and living holy lives. It focuses on offerings, priesthood, purity, and how to maintain a right relationship with God. The book emphasizes the importance of obedience and holiness among God's people.

Numbers.title: Numbers
Numbers.summary: Numbers tells the story of the Israelites’ journey from Mount Sinai to the Promised Land. It includes censuses, laws, and stories of their challenges and faithfulness. The book shows how God guided and tested His people along the way.

Deuteronomy.title: Deuteronomy
Deuteronomy.summary: Deuteronomy is a book where Moses reminds the Israelites of God's laws and promises before they enter the land of Canaan. It emphasizes obedience to God and staying faithful to His commandments. The book serves as a call to follow God's ways and trust in His guidance for the future.

Joshua.title: Joshua
Joshua.summary: The Book of Joshua tells how the Israelites, led by Joshua, entered and settled in the land of Canaan after Moses' death. It shows God's guidance and promises as they take cities and establish their nation. The book emphasizes faith, obedience, and God's faithfulness to His people.

Judges.title: Judges
Judges.summary: The book of Judges recounts the history of Israel from the time after Joshua’s leadership until they had kings. It shows a cycle where the people turn away from God, face difficulties, cry out for help, and then are delivered by leaders called judges. The book highlights both God's mercy and the consequences of turning away from Him.

Ruth.title: Ruth
Ruth.summary: The Book of Ruth tells the story of a woman named Ruth who shows loyalty and faithfulness to her mother-in-law Naomi and to God. It highlights themes of kindness, loyalty, and God's provision. Ruth’s story ultimately leads to her becoming part of the lineage of King David and Jesus.

1 Samuel.title: 1 Samuel
1 Samuel.summary: 1 Samuel tells the story of how Israel's leadership changed from the prophet Samuel to King Saul, and then to David. It shows important lessons about trusting God's guidance and the consequences of disobedience. The book highlights God's plans for leadership and His relationship with His people.

2 Samuel.title: 2 Samuel
2 Samuel.summary: 2 Samuel tells the story of King David's reign over Israel, including his successes and struggles. It highlights his victories in battle, his leadership, and the challenges he faced within his family and nation. The book shows both David's faith in God and the consequences of his actions.

1 Kings.title: 1 Kings
1 Kings.summary: 1 Kings tells the story of Israel's history after King David, focusing on Solomon's reign and the division of the kingdom into Israel and Judah. It highlights both the wise decisions and the mistakes made by the kings. The book shows how faithfulness or turning away from God affected the nation.

2 Kings.title: 2 Kings
2 Kings.summary: 2 Kings tells the story of the history of Israel and Judah after King David, focusing on the reigns of several kings and the prophets who guided them. It highlights how faithfulness to God leads to blessings, while disobedience often brings disaster. The book ends with the fall of Israel and Judah, showing the consequences of turning away from God.

1 Chronicles.title: 1 Chronicles
1 Chronicles.summary: 1 Chronicles is a book that traces the history of Israel, focusing on King David and efforts to organize worship and leadership. It emphasizes God's promises, the importance of worship, and God's plan through Jerusalem and David’s line. The book encourages faithfulness and remembrance of Israel's history.

2 Chronicles.title: 2 Chronicles
2 Chronicles.summary: 2 Chronicles continues the history of Israel, focusing on the southern kingdom of Judah. It highlights the importance of trusting God, following His commands, and the role of kings and priests in leading the people. The book encourages faithfulness to God to receive His blessings and avoid judgment.

Ezra.title: Ezra
Ezra.summary: Ezra tells the story of how the Israelites returned from exile in Babylon and began to rebuild Jerusalem and restore their worship. It emphasizes faithfulness to God's laws and renewing their identity as God's people. The book highlights the importance of obedience and community in following God's plan.

Nehemiah.title: Nehemiah
Nehemiah.summary: The Book of Nehemiah tells the story of how the Jewish people rebuild the walls of Jerusalem after returning from exile. Nehemiah leads the effort, showing strong leadership and faith in God. The book highlights the importance of prayer, teamwork, and trusting God during difficult times.

Esther.title: Esther
Esther.summary: The Book of Esther tells the story of a Jewish woman named Esther who becomes queen of Persia. When her people are in danger from a plots to destroy them, Esther bravely intervenes to save them. The book highlights themes of bravery, God's protection, and standing up for what is right.

Job.title: Job
Job.summary: The Book of Job tells the story of a man who experiences great suffering and questions why bad things happen to good people. It explores themes of faith, hardship, and God's justice. In the end, Job learns more about God's wisdom and his own trust in Him.

Psalms.title: Psalms
Psalms.summary: Psalms is a collection of poems and songs that express a wide range of emotions, from joy and thanks to sadness and asking for help. It helps readers connect with God through prayers, praise, and reflections on life. These writings have been used in worship and personal devotion for thousands of years.

Proverbs.title: Proverbs
Proverbs.summary: Proverbs is a book filled with wise sayings and advice for living a good life. It teaches how to be smart, kind, and respectful, emphasizing the importance of making wise choices. The book helps us understand how to act wisely in our daily lives and stay close to God.

Ecclesiastes.title: Ecclesiastes
Ecclesiastes.summary: Ecclesiastes is a book that explores the meaning of life and the things people often chase after to find happiness. The author reflects on the fleeting nature of wealth, pleasure, and success, and emphasizes the importance of trusting God. It encourages readers to find purpose in a life lived for Him.

Song of Solomon.title: Song of Solomon
Song of Solomon.summary: The Song of Solomon is a poetic book that celebrates love and the beauty of romantic relationship. It highlights the joy, attraction, and affection between a bride and her bridegroom, often seen as an allegory for God's love for His people. The book uses vivid imagery to express desire and admiration.

Isaiah.title: Isaiah
Isaiah.summary: Isaiah is a book that contains the words of the prophet Isaiah, who delivered messages from God to the people of Judah. It includes warnings about turning away from God and promises of hope and salvation. The book emphasizes God's holiness, justice, and plan for redemption through the coming Messiah.

Jeremiah.title: Jeremiah
Jeremiah.summary: Jeremiah was a prophet who delivered God's messages to the people of Judah, warning them about their sins and the coming destruction if they did not turn back to God. Despite the difficult messages, Jeremiah also shared God's promises of hope and a future renewed by Him. The book shows the importance of faithfulness and trusting God's plans even in tough times.

Lamentations.title: Lamentations
Lamentations.summary: Lamentations is a poetic book that expresses deep sorrow over the destruction of Jerusalem and the suffering of God's people. It acknowledges pain but also hopes for God's mercy and future restoration. The book reminds us that even in difficult times, God's compassion remains constant.

Ezekiel.title: Ezekiel
Ezekiel.summary: Ezekiel is a book filled with visions and messages from God given to the prophet Ezekiel during the time when the Israelites were in exile. It emphasizes God's holiness, judgment, and the hope of restoration for His people. The book encourages faithfulness and trust in God's plan even in difficult times.

Daniel.title: Daniel
Daniel.summary: The Book of Daniel tells the story of a Jewish young man who remains faithful to God while living in a foreign empire. It includes stories of faithfulness, dreams, and visions of future world events. The book encourages trust in God's plan even during difficult times.

Hosea.title: Hosea
Hosea.summary: Hosea is about God's love for His people, Israel, even when they turn away from Him. The book uses Hosea's own marriage as a symbol of Israel's unfaithfulness, showing God's desire to forgive and restore those who turn back to Him. It encourages people to return to God's love and mercy.

Joel.title: Joel
Joel.summary: The Book of Joel encourages God's people to turn back to Him and trust in His mercy during times of trouble. It speaks about a coming judgment, as well as the hope of renewal and blessing if they repent. The book emphasizes God's sovereignty and His desire for His people to seek Him sincerely.

Amos.title: Amos
Amos.summary: Amos was a prophet who spoke out against the injustice and corruption of the people of Israel and surrounding nations. He called for repentance and justice, warning of God's judgment if they did not turn back to Him. Despite this, Amos also shared God's hope for a future where people would seek Him and live righteously.

Obadiah.title: Obadiah
Obadiah.summary: Obadiah is a short book that talks about God's judgment against Edom, a nation that was proud and did wrong to Israel. It emphasizes that God's justice will happen and that everyone should live humbly before Him. The book also reminds us that God's plans include restoring His people after judgment.

Jonah.title: Jonah
Jonah.summary: The Book of Jonah tells the story of a prophet who tries to run away from God's command to go to the city of Nineveh and warn its people. Instead of escaping, Jonah is swallowed by a great fish and learns important lessons about God's mercy and compassion. In the end, Jonah understands that God's love extends to all people, even those who seem undeserving.

Micah.title: Micah
Micah.summary: Micah is a prophetic book that calls for justice and warns about the consequences of corruption and injustice. It emphasizes God's desire for kindness, justice, and humility among His people. Despite warnings of judgment, it also offers hope for a future where God's plan will be fulfilled.

Nahum.title: Nahum
Nahum.summary: Nahum is a short book in the Bible that predicts the fall of the city of Nineveh, the capital of Assyria, because of its cruelty and wickedness. It emphasizes God's justice and swears that evil will be punished. The book offers hope to God's people by showing that God's justice will ultimately prevail.

Habakkuk.title: Habakkuk
Habakkuk.summary: Habakkuk is a small book where the prophet asks God tough questions about justice and why evil seems to go unpunished. God responds by showing that He has a plan, even if it’s hard to understand. The book encourages trusting God’s wisdom and faithfulness during difficult times.

Zephaniah.title: Zephaniah
Zephaniah.summary: Zephaniah is a prophetic book that warns about God's coming judgment on Judah and other nations due to their wickedness. It also offers hope for those who turn back to God and trust in His mercy. The book encourages repentance and reminds readers of God's desire to restore and bless His people.

Haggai.title: Haggai
Haggai.summary: Haggai is a short book where the prophet encourages the people to rebuild the Jerusalem temple after returning from exile. It reminds them to prioritize God's house and trust in His promises. The book shows how obeying God can lead to blessings and His presence among His people.

Zechariah.title: Zechariah
Zechariah.summary: Zechariah is a book that contains visions and messages from God, delivered through the prophet Zechariah. It focuses on encouraging the Israelites to rebuild Jerusalem and stay faithful to God’s promises. The book also looks forward to a future Messianic king who will bring peace and salvation.

Malachi.title: Malachi
Malachi.summary: Malachi is the last book of the Old Testament and serves as a message from God to the people of Israel. It calls them to return to sincere worship, faithfulness, and proper attitudes toward God. The book also predicts the coming of a messenger who will prepare the way for the Lord.

Matthew.title: Gospel of Matthew
Matthew.summary: The Gospel of Matthew tells the story of Jesus Christ’s life, teachings, death, and resurrection. It emphasizes that Jesus is the promised Messiah who fulfills Old Testament prophecies. The book also encourages readers to follow Jesus’ teachings and live righteously.

Luke.title: Gospel of Luke
Luke.summary: The Gospel of Luke tells the story of Jesus Christ’s life, teachings, death, and resurrection. It emphasizes Jesus’ compassion, mercy, and the importance of caring for others. The book also highlights the role of women and the marginalized in Jesus’ ministry.

John.title: Gospel of John
John.summary: The Gospel of John tells the story of Jesus' life, teachings, death, and resurrection, emphasizing His divine nature as the Son of God. It highlights the importance of believing in Jesus to have eternal life. This book encourages readers to trust in Jesus and understand His love for the world.
keyThemes: Jesus is the Son of God

Acts.title: Acts
Acts.summary: The book of Acts tells the story of the early Christian church after Jesus' resurrection. It describes how the apostles spread the message of Jesus and how the church grew despite challenges. Acts shows the work of the Holy Spirit guiding believers everywhere.

Romans.title: Romans
Romans.summary: Romans is a letter that explains how people can be made right with God through faith in Jesus Christ. It teaches about God's grace, the importance of trusting Him, and how believers should live. The book emphasizes that salvation is available to everyone, no matter who they are.

1 Corinthians.title: 1 Corinthians
1 Corinthians.summary: 1 Corinthians is a letter from the Apostle Paul to the church in Corinth. It offers guidance on how Christians should live, worship, and work together in unity. The book addresses both spiritual practices and everyday issues faced by believers.

2 Corinthians.title: 2 Corinthians
2 Corinthians.summary: 2 Corinthians is a letter from the Apostle Paul to the church in Corinth, where he encourages them to stay strong in faith and trust God's grace. Paul also talks about his own challenges and the importance of living sincerely as followers of Jesus. The book emphasizes forgiveness, generosity, and the hope that comes from God's love.

Galatians.title: Galatians
Galatians.summary: Galatians is a letter where Paul writes to early Christians to remind them that faith in Jesus, not obeying the law, is what saves us. He encourages believers to live by the Spirit and not by trying to earn God's approval through rules. The book emphasizes freedom in Christ and loving one another.

Ephesians.title: Ephesians
Ephesians.summary: Ephesians was written by the Apostle Paul to encourage believers in how to live a life that reflects God's love and grace. It emphasizes that believers are part of God's plan and should walk in unity, love, and holiness. The book also reminds Christians of their identity in Christ and the spiritual blessings they have received.

Philippians.title: Philippians
Philippians.summary: Philippians is a letter from the Apostle Paul to the church in Philippi, encouraging believers to find joy in their faith regardless of their circumstances. It emphasizes the importance of humility, unity, and rejoicing in Christ. The book highlights how a strong relationship with Jesus can bring peace and happiness.

Colossians.title: Colossians
Colossians.summary: The Book of Colossians was written by the Apostle Paul to encourage the believers in Colossae to stay rooted in their faith in Jesus Christ. It emphasizes Christ’s supremacy and the importance of living a life that reflects His teachings. The letter also offers guidance on avoiding false teachings and spiritual deception.

1 Thessalonians.title: 1 Thessalonians
1 Thessalonians.summary: 1 Thessalonians is a letter from the Apostle Paul encouraging the church in Thessalonica. He praises their faith and love, and reminds them to stay strong and hopeful as they wait for Jesus to return. Paul also gives guidance on living a Christian life and supporting each other.

2 Thessalonians.title: 2 Thessalonians
2 Thessalonians.summary: 2 Thessalonians is a letter from the Apostle Paul to encourage the church in Thessalonica. He reminds believers to stay faithful, work hard, and remain hopeful for Jesus' return. Paul also warns against false teachings and encourages prayer and perseverance.

1 Timothy.title: 1 Timothy
1 Timothy.summary: 1 Timothy is a letter from the Apostle Paul to Timothy, offering guidance on how to lead and manage churches. It provides instructions on church leadership, proper behavior, and staying faithful to Christian teachings. The book emphasizes the importance of living a godly life and remaining committed to Jesus.

2 Timothy.title: 2 Timothy
2 Timothy.summary: 2 Timothy is a letter from the Apostle Paul to his friend Timothy. In it, Paul encourages Timothy to stay faithful to Jesus, even when things are difficult. He reminds him to keep teaching the truth and live a life that shows his faith.

Titus.title: Titus
Titus.summary: The Book of Titus is a letter from the Apostle Paul to a young church leader named Titus. It offers instructions on church leadership, proper behavior, and the importance of living a good example of faith. The letter emphasizes grace, sound teaching, and living a Christ-centered life.

Philemon.title: Philemon
Philemon.summary: The Book of Philemon is a letter from the Apostle Paul that appeals to a Christian brother, Philemon, to forgive and accept Onesimus, a former slave, as a brother in Christ. It emphasizes forgiveness, reconciliation, and the value of every person in God's eyes. Paul encourages Philemon to show love and kindness, reflecting Christ's love for all believers.

Hebrews.title: Hebrews
Hebrews.summary: Hebrews encourages believers to trust in Jesus Christ as the ultimate and perfect way to connect with God. It explains that Jesus is greater than all previous ways God revealed Himself and calls believers to stay faithful. The book emphasizes perseverance and faith in Jesus amid challenges.

James.title: James
James.summary: The Book of James encourages Christians to live out their faith through good works, wisdom, and perseverance. It emphasizes the importance of controlling the tongue, caring for others, and staying faithful during tough times. Overall, it calls believers to demonstrate their faith through actions.

1 Peter.title: 1 Peter
1 Peter.summary: 1 Peter encourages Christians to remain strong in their faith despite facing suffering and opposition. It teaches about living a holy life, trusting in God's grace, and remaining hopeful for eternal salvation. The book offers comfort and guidance for believers to stay faithful in difficult times.

2 Peter.title: 2 Peter
2 Peter.summary: 2 Peter encourages believers to remain faithful and watchful for false teachings that can threaten their faith. The book reminds Christians of the importance of growing in knowledge of Jesus and living holy lives. It also warns about the consequences of turning away from the truth.

1 John.title: 1 John
1 John.summary: 1 John is a letter that encourages believers to live in love and truth, emphasizing the importance of knowing Jesus Christ. It helps Christians understand how to grow in faith and stay faithful to God's teachings. The book also warns against false teachings and reminds believers of the importance of loving one another.

2 John.title: 2 John
2 John.summary: 2 John is a short letter where the author encourages believers to live in love and truth. It warns against false teachers who might lead people away from the core teachings of Jesus. The letter also emphasizes the importance of staying true to the message of Christ as we show love to each other.

3 John.title: 3 John
3 John.summary: 3 John is a short letter from the Apostle John that encourages believers to show hospitality and support faithful teachers. It warns against harmful behavior and promotes kindness and truth. The main focus is on living in love and standing for what is right.

Jude.title: Jude
Jude.summary: Jude is a short letter that warns believers to stay true to their faith and be careful of false teachers who can lead people away from God. It encourages Christians to remain strong and God-focused. The book emphasizes the importance of good deeds and trusting God's judgment.

Revelation.title: Revelation
Revelation.summary: Revelation is the last book of the Bible and describes visions of the future, including the final victory of good over evil. It offers hope and encouragement to Christians facing struggles, reminding them that God’s plan will ultimately be fulfilled. The book uses vivid imagery and symbolism to show God’s sovereignty and the promise of eternal life with Him.
